title: 1-s2.0-S0360319924025898-main
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
Here's the technical information extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   None Explicitly

**2. Graphs/Charts: NO_VISUAL_CONTENT**

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 1: Summary of the non-intrusive diagnostic techniques.**
    *   **Headers:** Technique, Primary Applications, Advantages, Disadvantage
    *   **Data (Technique, Primary Applications, Advantages, Disadvantage):**
        *   **PLIF (Planar Laser-Induced Fluorescence):**  Measurement of flow field and concentration distribution; High spatial resolution, can measure instantaneous concentration and temperature fields; Requires fluorescent dye, may affect fluid dynamics, complex data processing
        *   **VLIF (Volumetric Laser-Induced Fluorescence):** 3D temperature and concentration distribution in flow fields, 3D imaging of combustion processes; 3D imaging capability, detailed spatial distribution, high resolution; Complex optical system, large computational load, expensive instruments
        *   **PIV (Particle Image Velocimetry):** Fluid velocity field measurement, turbulence studies, aerodynamic experiments; Non-intrusive, high precision velocity measurement, applicable to a wide range of fluid dynamics applications; Requires particle tracers, complex imaging system, certain environmental requirements
        *   **Tomo-PIV (Tomographic PIV):** 3D fluid velocity field measurement, analysis of complex flows, 3D structure of turbulence; Provides 3D velocity information, suitable for complex and turbulent flows, high temporal resolution; Complex data processing, difficult system calibration, expensive equipment
        *   **Laser Raman Scattering:** Measurement of chemical composition and temperature, combustion process analysis, gas composition detection; Non-intrusive, high selectivity and high resolution, suitable for multi-component analysis; Weak signal, requires high-power laser, complex data processing and interpretation
        *   **TDLAS (Tunable Diode Laser Absorption Spectroscopy):** Measurement of gas composition and concentration, pollution monitoring, industrial process control; High sensitivity and selectivity, real-time measurement, suitable for quantitative analysis of various gas components; Sensitive to flow velocity and temperature, limited laser tuning range, significant environmental interference
        *   **TDLAT (Tunable Diode Laser Absorption Tomography):** 3D distribution measurement of gas composition and temperature, combustion studies, pollution source tracking; 3D imaging capability, high sensitivity and selectivity, real-time monitoring; Complex system, large computational load for data processing and imaging, difficult optical alignment and calibration

**4. Diagrams: NO_VISUAL_CONTENT**

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Laser Wavelengths: 355 nm, 431 nm, 532 nm, 309.24 nm
*   Grating: 2400 1/mm
*   Camera Resolutions: 1024 x 1024 pixels, 512 x 512 pixels
*   Laser Pulse Frequency: 10 Hz
*   Pulse Energy: 10 mJ/pulse
*   Target Excitation Wavelength: 314.415 nm
*   Pulse Duration: ~10 ns
*   Intensifier Gating Time: 100 ns
*   Focal Length of Lenses: 200 mm, 300 mm, 250 mm
